name: libdbi-perl
title: libdbi-perl
description:
Perl Database Interface (DBI)
DBI (DataBase Interface) is a Perl framework that provides a common interface
to access various backend databases in a uniform manner. DBD (DataBase Driver)
modules provide implementations for various backend data storage mechanisms
including networked relational databases (particularly SQL databases) and even
web services such as the Google search engine.
.
It is extremely portable and available for a wide range of operating systems,
architectures and data stores, including:
.
* Oracle
* Microsoft SQL Server
* IBM DB2
* SQLite
* PostgreSQL
* Firebird
* MySQL

commands.name: dbilogstrip
commands.description: filter to normalize DBI trace logs for diff'ing

arguments.description: Replaces any hex addresses, e.g, 0x128f72ce with "0xN".
arguments.raw: -u  Replaces any hex addresses, e.g, 0x128f72ce with "0xN".

commands.name: dbiproxy
commands.description: A proxy server for the DBD::Proxy driver

commands.name: dh_perl_dbi
commands.description: add dependencies required for DBI modules

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use dbilogstrip because Perl Database Interface (DBI).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use dbilogstrip as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `dbilogstrip dbitrace.log > dbitrace_stripped.log` as documented upstream for dbilogstrip.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair dbilogstrip with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `dbilogstrip --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

arguments.name: -u
arguments.description: Replaces any hex addresses, e.g, 0x128f72ce with "0xN".

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install libdbi-perl. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `dbilogstrip dbitrace.log > dbitrace_stripped.log`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Perl Database Interface (DBI).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: dbilogstrip - filter to normalize DBI trace logs for diff'ing.
